request.question:
Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.
In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

hypothesis.intervention: conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
hypothesis.threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin; reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: doxorubicin folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles HeLa intracellular accumulation cytotoxicity
hypothesis.original_hypothesis:
Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.
In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

references.relevance_note: This paper specifically discusses doxorubicin-loaded folate-functionalized nanoparticles, which directly aligns with the proposed intervention in the hypothesis.

Other studies on similar nanoparticles suggest comparable methodologies (e.g., papers [4] and [5]).

plan.objective: To determine the intracellular drug accumulation and cytotoxicity of doxorubicin conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be treated with doxorubicin-loaded folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles and free doxorubicin. Drug accumulation will be measured using fluorescence spectrophotometry, and cytotoxicity will be assessed using a MTT assay in both HeLa and negative control HEK293 cells.
controls: Free doxorubicin at a concentration of 0.1 µM in culture medium.
Doxorubicin-free control group in both HeLa and HEK293 cells.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Dissolve 100 mg of poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) (PLGA) in 5 mL of dichloromethane (DCM) at room temperature to prepare PLGA solution.
Expected outcome: Homogeneous PLGA solution obtained.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Add 1 mL of a 10 mg/mL doxorubicin solution in DMSO dropwise to the PLGA solution, stirring vigorously for 30 min to enable conjugation.
Expected outcome: Doxorubicin conjugated to PLGA is mixed uniformly.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Add 20 mL of 0.1% polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) solution to the PLGA-doxorubicin mixture and sonicate for 10 min at 40 kHz to form nanoparticles.
Expected outcome: Formation of PLGA-doxorubicin nanoparticles.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Nanoparticle Purification
Centrifuge the nanoparticle suspension at 12,000 × g for 15 min at 4°C and discard the supernatant to remove unbound doxorubicin.
Expected outcome: Purified nanoparticle pellet formed.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Nanoparticle Resuspension
Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) to obtain a final concentration suitable for cell treatment.
Expected outcome: Nanoparticles are resuspended in PBS with no visible aggregates.

DAY 1 | 48 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in 10 mL of RPMI-1640 medium supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) at 37°C in 5% CO2 until 70% confluence is reached.
Expected outcome: HeLa cells achieve 70% confluence.

DAY 1 | 4 h | Cell Treatment
Treat HeLa cells with 100 µL of either doxorubicin-loaded nanoparticles (100 µg/mL) or free doxorubicin (0.1 µM) in duplicate in 24-well plates for 4 h at 37°C.
Expected outcome: Doxorubicin internalization into HeLa cells observed.

DAY 1 | 4 h | Cytotoxicity Assessment
Transfer the medium and add 100 µL of MTT solution (5 mg/mL in PBS) to each well and incubate for 3 h at 37°C to assess cell viability.
Expected outcome: Colorimetric change in viable cells at 575 nm.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Fluorescence Measurement
Measure the fluorescence of doxorubicin-treated cells at an excitation wavelength of 480 nm and emission wavelength of 590 nm using a Biotek Synergy H1 plate reader.
Expected outcome: Intracellular drug levels quantified through fluorescence intensity.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Analyze the MTT assay and fluorescence data using GraphPad Prism to calculate viability percentages and drug accumulation in cells.
Expected outcome: Determine intracellular accumulation is at least 3-fold higher in nanoparticle-treated cells vs. free doxorubicin.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Folate receptors are overexpressed in HeLa cells leading to enhanced uptake of folate-functionalized nanoparticles.
Cell viability and drug accumulation can be quantified accurately using MTT assays and fluorescence spectrometry.
Experimental conditions can be replicated consistently across assays.
decision_criteria: Proceed if intracellular doxorubicin accumulation in HeLa cells is ≥3-fold higher than free doxorubicin.
Cytotoxicity in HEK293 cells is reduced by at least 40% compared to free doxorubicin.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Standardize PLGA batch number across experiments.
Use the same MTT reagent lot number for each assay.
Confirm the concentration of doxorubicin in each batch before use.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin; reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40% improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the intracellular drug accumulation and cytotoxicity of doxorubicin conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
